Technology.thesis: The AI infrastructure buildout continues to be the dominant driver, with strong demand for semiconductors, hardware, and related software. Enterprise software is also seeing a boost from AI integration, though some areas face valuation scrutiny.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
China export controls expand to HBM and advanced packaging — would disrupt supply chains
Global chip sales growth decelerates below 15% YoY — signals cooling demand

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: Retail is seeing a mixed bag, with discount stores and e-commerce platforms showing resilience or growth, while some apparel and restaurant brands face headwinds. Residential construction shows resilience despite higher rates.
watchFor: Consumer spending data (retail sales) shows significant decline > 1% MoM
Interest rates (30Y mortgage) break above 8% — would cool housing demand
Major retailers (WMT, COST) issue negative forward guidance

Communication Services.thesis: Internet content and entertainment companies are seeing growth driven by new strategies and strong user engagement, but some areas like electronic gaming face mixed sentiment.
watchFor: Major advertising spending cuts by corporations — would hit content platforms
Streaming subscriber growth decelerates across the board
New regulatory pressures on social media or gaming platforms

Industrials.thesis: Aerospace & Defense is benefiting from geopolitical tensions and government contracts, while Farm & Heavy Construction Machinery faces headwinds. Integrated Freight & Logistics is navigating a complex environment with strategic shifts.
watchFor: Major de-escalation in geopolitical conflicts — would reduce defense spending
Global trade volumes decline significantly — would hurt freight companies
Interest rate hikes impact capital expenditure for heavy machinery

Healthcare.thesis: The GLP-1 obesity drug wave continues to drive strong performance for leading pharmaceutical companies, while healthcare plans show resilience. Medical devices face mixed sentiment.
watchFor: New GLP-1 competitors emerge with superior efficacy or fewer side effects
Regulatory backlash against high GLP-1 drug pricing
Healthcare plans face unexpected increases in medical loss ratios

Financials.thesis: Diversified banks and credit services are showing strength with projected revenue growth and attractive valuations. Asset management and capital markets are also seeing positive momentum.
watchFor: Unexpected interest rate cuts by the Fed — would compress net interest margins for banks
Significant increase in loan defaults across consumer and corporate segments
Regulatory changes impacting bank profitability (e.g., increased capital requirements)

Consumer Defensive.thesis: The consumer defensive sector is seeing mixed signals, with some discount retailers showing resilience while household and personal products face specific challenges.
watchFor: Unexpected surge in inflation impacting consumer purchasing power
Significant shift in consumer preferences away from discount stores
Major supply chain disruptions for household goods

Utilities.thesis: Independent power producers and renewable energy companies are seeing positive analyst attention and strong performance, driven by strategic initiatives and market momentum.
watchFor: Sudden policy shifts away from renewable energy incentives
Significant increase in natural gas prices impacting power generation costs
Interest rate hikes making capital-intensive utility projects more expensive

Basic Materials.thesis: The industrial metals and mining sector is facing mixed signals, with some companies experiencing volatility due to geopolitical factors and valuation concerns.
watchFor: Global economic slowdown impacting industrial demand for metals
New tariffs or trade restrictions on raw materials
MP Materials (MP) reports declining rare earth prices

Precious Metals.thesis: Gold is holding steady with debates around its valuation and future price targets, while silver faces downward pressure due to weakening demand.
watchFor: Federal Reserve signals aggressive rate hikes — would strengthen dollar and hurt metals
Significant de-escalation of global geopolitical tensions — would reduce safe-haven demand
US Dollar Index (DXY) breaks above 108 — would weigh on dollar-denominated commodities
